data.title: Private AV Startups are About to Take Over U.S. Streets—Here's How Cities Will Change in 2026
data.description:
On Feb. 11, 2026, Morgan Stanley (MS) released a research report calling 2026 an inflection year for private autonomous vehicle (AV) companies. The firm said privately backed operators are expanding into additional U.S. cities and accelerating commercial deployment.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Private AV Companies Expand Across U.S. Cities

According to Morgan Stanley Research, autonomous vehicle services, largely operated by private companies, are now active in eight major U.S. cities after more than two decades of development. The firm expects acceleration in 2026, driven mainly by private AV operators, with 33 consumer launches and expansion into nine additional cities. "2026 is set to be a year for inflection," said Brian Nowak, Head of U.S. Internet Research at Morgan Stanley. 

Morgan Stanley estimates AV miles driven in the U.S. reached 116 million in 2025 and will grow at an average annual rate of 103% through 2032, reaching about 16 billion miles. Even then, AV miles would account for roughly 0.5% of total U.S. miles driven and about 30% of rideshare miles. For investors, Nowak has said that many will closely monitor those metrics to see which companies are moving fastest. He noted that both private and public players, including vehicle manufacturers, sensor and camera makers, software developers, and rideshare companies, are scaling production and forming partnerships.

Private Companies Firms Expand Beyond AVs

Morgan Stanley's projected acceleration in the deployment of self-driving cars is already reflected in funding activity among private AV startups. Waabi, the U.S.-based autonomous trucking company, recently secured $750 million to commercialize its self-driving technology and an additional $250 million in milestone funding from Uber (UBER) to expand into robotaxis. Separately, WeRide (WRD) and Uber launched their first commercial robotaxi service in downtown Abu Dhabi, advancing the scale of urban deployment by private AV operators.

For investors interested, Morgan Stanley Research also identified sectors where private companies are driving transformative change beyond AVs. The firm cited drone technology, operational technology, cybersecurity, AI-powered healthcare tools, and space-based infrastructure as areas gaining momentum. The report added that private capital remains elevated, with $4.3 trillion in dry powder at the end of 2026, and that 2025 take-private activity ranked second-highest in the past decade.

Where to Invest in Autonomous Vehicles?

data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Will Silver Surge to $200 in 2026?
data.description:
Silver (XAGUSD) has already rallied 15% this year, outperforming both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) by a wide margin. The precious metal has benefited from a trifecta of geopolitical uncertainty, a weakening dollar, and persistent inflation. That's left some investors wondering if silver will be able to reach $200 by the end of 2026.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Most analysts believe $200 is out of reach, although they don't completely rule out the possibility. "Achieving $200 silver by 2026 is certainly possible but under extraordinary conditions and not necessarily based on current trends," said Birch Gold Group financial market strategist Peter Reagan.

Silver Rally Faces Dollar and Substitution Risks

Reagan added that the ingredients necessary for a strong silver rally include strong industrial demand, a supply-demand imbalance, and rising investment demand. Threats to the precious metal include producers turning to silver alternatives and a stronger dollar.

First Information CEO Vince Stanzione sees a path to $200 before the next presidential election in November 2028, although he warned that the scenario would likely coincide with a significant devaluation of currencies.

data.title: Oracle Stock (ORCL) Rises on New U.S. Air Force Cloud Deal
data.description:
Oracle's (ORCL) shares turned positive on Wednesday after the software giant notched an $88 million cloud contract with the U.S. Air Force.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Texas-based company's shares have only returned to green territory in the last two days. Investors recently sold off shares in the company over concerns about its weaker revenue growth, despite cloud revenue jumping 34% to $8 billion at the end of the second quarter of fiscal 2026 that ended in November.

In a statement, Oracle noted that its new contract with the U.S. Air Force will see the company deliver its Oracle Cloud Infrastructure services to Cloud One, the U.S. Air Force's centralized cloud-hosting program. The deal also reinforces Oracle's role as a key partner in the U.S. Department of Defense's cloud modernization efforts, the firm noted.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Micron Stock (MU): Why Is This Semiconductor Stock Up Today, 2/12/26?
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) traded higher early Thursday, extending a 10% jump from Wednesday after management provided an update on its latest AI memory chips. At an investor event, Chief Financial Officer Mark Murphy said Micron has begun shipping its newest high-bandwidth memory chips, called HBM4, to customers. The company is already in large-scale production and expects shipments to grow this quarter — about one quarter earlier than it had told investors in December.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It's important to note that HBM4 chips work alongside AI processors and help them move data quickly, making them a key part of AI servers.

Murphy also said demand for this type of memory remains much higher than supply and could stay tight beyond 2026. Such supply shortages usually keep prices firm and support profit margins for memory makers.

Micron Moves Closer to Rivals

Micron had been seen as trailing rivals Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK Hynix in bringing the newest memory to market. That raised concerns that it could miss large orders from AI chip makers such as Nvidia (NVDA).

The new timeline eased those worries. Investors now see Micron as entering the AI supply chain sooner than expected, which could improve revenue growth over the next year.

Is MU a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MU stock based on 27 Buys, two Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average MU price target of $395.30 per share implies 3.67% downside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: S&P 500 Advances as U.S. Dials Back China Tech Restrictions
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading higher after the U.S. agreed to scale back several tech restrictions against China, according to Reuters.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The U.S. has halted proposed restrictions covering TP-Link router sales, the U.S. operations of China Unicom and China Mobile, and Chinese electric truck and bus sales, according to four people familiar with the situation. In return, China has committed to delay restrictions on rare-earths, which are crucial in the development of semiconductors, solar panels, and defense systems.

Eased Tech Restrictions on China Place Security in Focus

The softer tech restrictions likely act as a tool to cool trade tensions between the two sides following a trade truce reached last October. However, some experts warn that the move could harm U.S. national security. Matt Pottinger, a deputy national security advisor during President Trump's first term, warned that it's "ironic that we're actually letting Beijing acquire new areas of leverage over the U.S. economy – in telecoms infrastructure, in data centers and AI, and EVs."

The Trump administration was hesitant to enforce the paused restrictions last year, with the Commerce Department shifting its focus to tech threats from Iran and Russia following the trade truce. However, the department could implement some of the measures if U.S.-China relations deteriorate after Trump's meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping in April.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Is Icon Stock (ICLR) Down Today?
data.description:
Icon (ICLR) stock took a beating on Thursday after the clinical research company announced the delay of its Q4 2025 earnings report. The company now expects to release its most recent earnings report on or before April 30, 2026.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Icon's delayed earnings report announcement came alongside the revelation of an ongoing internal investigation initiated in October 2025. This investigation is being conducted by the Audit Committee of the Board of Directors and is focused on the company's accounting practices and controls. The investigation was started due to concerns reported to the Audit Committee and outside legal counsel is also conducting its own investigation with the assistance of forensic and technical accounting firms.

Ciaran Murray, Chairman of the Icon Board of Directors, said, "The ICON Board of Directors is committed to transparency, accountability and strong governance. The Board of Directors and I have full faith in ICON's executive team and their ability to implement the changes needed as a result of the investigation and execute our strategy for the years ahead."

Icon Stock Movement Today

Icon stock was down 33.15% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 7.89% fall yesterday. The shares have also decreased 26.93% year-to-date and 27.1% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came strong early-morning trading of ICLR stock, as some 557,000 shares changed hands. This is over half of the stock's three-month daily average trading volume, and that activity occurred before today's opening bell.
data.author: William White

data.title: NVDA vs. AVGO: Which AI Chip Stock Analysts Prefer Ahead of Earnings?
data.description:
Chipmakers Nvidia (NVDA) and Broadcom (AVGO) are two of the biggest winners of the AI boom, powering everything from data centers to advanced AI models. As both companies gear up to report quarterly earnings, investors are watching closely to see which is better positioned to capitalize on surging AI demand. With valuations running high and expectations even higher, the upcoming results could serve as a key catalyst for both stocks.

NVDA or AVGO: A Quick Comparison

data.title: Anthropic Fires $20 Million to Blast the Billionaire-Backed AI Lobby
data.description:
Anthropic is jumping into politics today, with a massive $20 million donation to the advocacy group Public First. This move sets up a direct fight in the 2026 midterm races between Anthropic's "safety-first" vision and a rival group of Silicon Valley heavyweights. While companies like OpenAI and venture firm Andreessen Horowitz are spending record sums to stop what they call "onerous" rules, Anthropic is betting that voters want leaders who will put guardrails on the rapidly evolving technology.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Anthropic Creates a Safety Fund

The donation targets the influence of Leading the Future, a super PAC backed by OpenAI's Greg Brockman and Andreessen Horowitz that plans to spend $125 million to fill Congress with allies who want lighter rules. Anthropic, which has long marketed its Claude models as more ethical than its competitors, is no longer willing to let its rivals make all the rules. "We don't want to sit on the sidelines while these policies are developed," the company stated in a blog post Thursday, arguing that the U.S. needs "flexible regulation that allows us to reap the benefits of AI [and] keep the risks in check."

Public First Targets Key Midterm Races

Public First Action is already using the money to support candidates who want strict oversight. The group launched ad campaigns this week for Senator Marsha Blackburn in her run for Tennessee governor and Senator Pete Ricketts in Nebraska. The ads for Blackburn talk about her history with children's online safety, while the Ricketts campaign focuses on keeping advanced AI chips away from foreign adversaries. This strategy tries to show that AI regulation is about national security and the public interest rather than just slowing down innovation.

The Fight for AI's Future Intensifies

The fight for the future of AI is intensifying as 700 AI bills currently move through state legislatures, making the 2026 elections a battle over whether technology should be governed by the companies that build it or the politicians who worry about it. The opposition remains formidable because Leading the Future has already spent hundreds of thousands of dollars to defeat AI safety advocates. The group is using its massive budget to promote an innovation argument. Moreover, they claim that state-level regulations create a patchwork of rules that could bankrupt smaller startups.

Investors can track private companies like Anthropic on TipRanks. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani
